attributes.description: Alerts when `rabbitmq.node.mem_alarm > 0`. The node has raised the memory alarm — producers are blocked.
attributes.name: [RabbitMQ OTel] Memory alarm triggered

investigation_guide.blob:
## RabbitMQ memory alarm triggered

### What fired
`rabbitmq.node.mem_alarm > 0` during the window.

### Why it matters
When memory use exceeds the configured watermark, RabbitMQ blocks publishers. Publishers will appear to hang until memory is released.

### Triage
1. Check queue length and memory per queue.
2. Look for unacked messages holding memory.

### Remediation
- Drain large queues / kill stuck consumers.
- Raise `vm_memory_high_watermark`.

### Tuning
- Always P1.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-rabbitmqreceiver.otel-default
// Memory alarm: when triggered (1), producers are blocked
// Requires node-level metrics enabled in receiver config
| WHERE metrics.rabbitmq.node.mem_alarm IS NOT NULL
  AND metrics.rabbitmq.node.mem_alarm > 0
| STATS alarm_count = COUNT(*)
  BY resource.attributes.rabbitmq.node.name
| WHERE alarm_count > 0
